name: Riboflavin (Vitamin B2)
contraindications: No major toxicity at typical use levels
High-dose migraine use differs from dietary dosing

sources.title: Vitamin B2 Fact Sheet for Health Professionals (NIH ODS)

description: Required to prevent ariboflavinosis; migraine studies use pharmacologic doses higher than RDA intake.
summary: Essential B2 vitamin for FMN/FAD coenzymes; high-dose migraine use is distinct from nutrition dosing.
mechanism: Supports redox enzymes via FMN/FAD; migraine benefit is a proposed mitochondrial-energy mechanism.
safetyNotes: Bright yellow urine can occur and is benign.
interactions: No major consistent drug interactions established.
dosage: Adult RDA about 1.1–1.3 mg/day; migraine trials often use ~400 mg/day.
regulatoryStatus: Essential nutrient with dietary reference intakes.
